title: Dye sensitized photoactive surfaces
abstract: Upon light irradiation, a dye sensitized photoactive surfaces can undergo hydrophobic to hydrophilic conversion in both in air and oil environments.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A surface of a solid-liquid interface, the surface modified with a composition comprising: a matrix including a plurality of particles, wherein a surface of the particles is modified with a photosensitizer, wherein the wettability of the surface changes upon irradiation with light. 2. The surface of claim 1, wherein the plurality of particles include a metal oxide. 3. The surface of claim 2, wherein the metal oxide includes Ti0 2 , ZnO, W0 3 , SrTi0 3 , Sn0 2 , V 2 0 5 , Ce0 2 , CuO, Mo0 3 , Fe 2 0 3 , Cr 2 0 3 or ln 2 0 3 . 4. The surface of claim 2, wherein the particle is a nanoparticle. 5. The surface of claim 1, wherein the matrix includes a multilayer including: a first layer including a polyelectrolyte polymer; and a second layer including the plurality of particles in contact with the first layer. 6. The surface of claim 4, wherein the polyelectrolyte polymer includes poly(allylamine hydrochloride). 7. The surface of claim 1, wherein the photosensitizer includes Chlorin. 8. The surface of claim 1, wherein the photosensitizer includes a Ruthenium (II) polypyridyl complex. 9. The surface of claim 1, wherein the photosensitizer is N3, D149, Chlorin, Coumarin 343, Eosin Y, N719, N621, N179, N749, N945, Z907, Z907-Na, Z910, Z675, Z646, Z991, K8, K19, K20, K51, K60, K68, K73, K77, D5, D6, D102, D205, D29, D35, D37, DPP07, DPP13, DPP 14, DPP 15, DPP 17, Al, A2, A3, A597, ClOl, C102, C217, CYC-B l, CYC-B3, CYC-Bl l, YS-1, YS-2, YS-3, YS-4, YS-5, YE05, Y123, YD2, YD2-0-C8, T18, T66, TFRS-1, TFRS-2, TFRS-3, HRS-1, SJW-E1, S8, JK-91, JK-92, LI17, HY2, DX1, SM371, SM315, RK1, KX-2569, 

 MB18-N, LEG4, DEKl, B18, D45, D51, D77, C106, N820, N823, N886, K9, N712, NKK-2553, NKK-2554, TG6, JF419, MKA253, LI, MK245, HSQ4, or PI . 10. The surface of claim 1, wherein the particles are covalently bonded to the photosensitizer. 11. The surface of claim 1, wherein the solid includes glass, fiber, stainless steel, inorganic materials or sand. 12. The surface of claim 11, wherein the solid is coated with indium tin oxide. 13. An oil-water separation device including a membrane comprising the surface of claim 1. 14. A method of separating oil from water in an oil-water mixture comprising: contacting a surface of an oil-water separation device with the oil-water mixture, the surface, the surface modified with a composition comprising a matrix including a plurality of nanoparticles, wherein a surface of the particles are modified with a photosensitizer, wherein the wettability of the surface changes upon irradiation with light; and irradiating the surface with light. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the plurality of particles include a metal oxide. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the metal oxide includes Ti0 2 , ZnO, W0 3 , SrTi0 3 , Sn0 2 , V 2 0 5 , Ce0 2 , CuO, M0O 3 , Fe 2 0 3 , Cr 2 0 3 or ln 2 0 3 . 17. The method of claim 14, wherein the photosensitizer includes Chlorin. 18. The method of claim 14, wherein the photosensitizer includes a Ruthenium (II) polypyridyl complex. 19. A method of coating a surface of an oil-water separating device comprising 

 depositing a matrix including a plurality of particles on the surface, wherein a surface of the particles are modified with a photosensitizer, wherein the wettability of the surface changes upon irradiation with light. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the plurality of particles include a metal oxide.
